mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is firm and FEDFUNDS is still near zero, investors infer that inflation pressure will force earlier policy normalization. That pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days through rising term-premium and front-end rate expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if 10-year yield falls in most 20-day windows after CPIAUCSL remains elevated while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%, or if the sign hit rate drops below 40% across triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration and weaker inflation pressure. Over the next 20 trading days this lowers expected policy rates and pulls the 10-year Treasury yield down as bond investors price slower nominal growth.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp versus the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if yield rises in more than 60% of cases when UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp monthly increase threshold, or if the average 20-day yield change is non-negative after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer demand and improved nominal growth expectations. That lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the next month because markets price more resilient growth and less need for prolonged accommodation.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum is followed by negative or flat 20-day yield changes in a majority of observations, or if rolling 3-year sign accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO turns down while HOUST also weakens, the economy is losing real activity in both industrial and housing channels. That combination lowers inflation expectations and reduces the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month and HOUST declines month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous monthly declines in INDPRO and HOUST do not correspond to lower 20-day yields more often than not, or if the interaction adds no predictive value versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in PAYEMS confirms labor demand is improving, which supports stronger nominal growth expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over a 60-day horizon. The effect is strongest when unemployment is not rising at the same time.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS gains fail to precede higher yields over 60 trading days, or if the signal disappears when UNRATE is flat or falling, indicating no labor-demand transmission.

mechanism_narrative: If FEDFUNDS is already above 4.5%, additional policy tightening is interpreted as restrictive. Over the next 20 trading days that compresses growth expectations and lowers the 10-year Treasury yield as recession odds rise.
failure_condition: Reject if, after FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the 20-day yield change is positive in a majority of cases or the conditional average yield change is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is paired with firm retail sales, the market reads this as real-demand acceleration rather than transitory noise. That combination should raise the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days via stronger growth and inflation pricing.
failure_condition: Reject if synchronized improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not precede higher 60-day yields, or if one proxy alone explains the result and the joint interaction is unnecessary.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led slowdown, falling HOUST signals weaker construction demand and softer downstream inflation. Over the next 5 trading days this should pull the 10-year Treasury yield down as investors extend duration.
failure_condition: Reject if large monthly declines in HOUST are followed by higher or unchanged 5-day yields in most instances, or if the effect is indistinguishable from noise in rolling out-of-sample tests.
